name: Portal SSO and tenant-safe Meeting administration
scope: Portal D1 hashed handoff and session persistence
Live Portal account and platform-owner authorization verification
Friendly protected Meeting console login and forbidden pages
Portal new-user AM feature selection persistence
requiresDatabases: Existing Portal admin_users with AM tenant authorization fields
Existing target-local Group Bindings required by AM-IMP-2026.0721.01

dataIsolation.guard: Install code and additive schema independently. Portal accounts, token hashes, sessions, tenant permissions, Group Bindings, Meeting records, LINE identities, Notion data, credentials, and deployment values remain inside the target environment and tenant.

changes: Adds D1-backed, hashed, 60-second, single-use Portal handoffs
Adds D1-backed, hashed, tenant-bound, fixed eight-hour AM sessions
Requires current active Portal owner on each session verification
Adds allowlisted local return-path handling that overwrites tenant context
Allows an exact protected Meeting console GET to render a friendly denied page while APIs remain centrally blocked
Reclassifies the Meeting management console as a tenant administration route
Requires isPlatformOwner for every Meeting console page and API operation
Limits the Meeting management console to platform owners
Persists AM tenant feature selections when a Portal administrator creates a user
definitionOfDone: Portal D1 contains additive am_sso_handoffs and am_sso_sessions tables and required indexes
Raw handoff and session tokens never enter D1 or browser-visible logs
Handoff consumption is tenant-bound, expiry-checked, conditional, and exactly once
Session verification is tenant-bound, expiry-checked, non-sliding, and reloads the current active Portal owner
AM Platform health reports Portal service authentication configured without exposing its value
Unauthenticated exact Meeting console GET renders a friendly Portal login page and protected API paths remain 401 JSON
Selected-group users cannot read or mutate the Meeting console and receive 403
Tenant-wide non-owner managers cannot read or mutate the Meeting console and receive 403
Platform owners may use authorized tenant tabs
Only allowlisted local return paths survive SSO and the consumed tenant always replaces a supplied tenant query
Portal new-user creation preserves all selected AM tenant features
Portal and AM Platform verification suites pass
Each target keeps its own secrets, D1 rows, accounts, sessions, Group Bindings, Meeting data, and deployment record
The target manifest and project-local upgrade record are updated after verification
Production is marked Deployed only after a real account completes SSO, safe return, live revocation, and Meeting authorization smoke tests
